perItemFindings.label: lem:le-cam-two-point-chisq
perItemFindings.one_line: The cited decl Causalean.Stat.two_point_lower_bound_of_chiSqDiv_le is a real-valued estimator miss-probability bound, concluding a lower bound on max(P0{|est-θ0|≥s}, P1{|est-θ1|≥s}); the core node needs a two-hypothesis testing lemma for every test psi with error-sum P_+(psi≠+)+P_-(psi≠-) bounded below by a positive c(C_chi).

review.verbatim_critique: Re-plan lem:le-cam-two-point-chisq. Drop direct reuse of Causalean.Stat.two_point_lower_bound_of_chiSqDiv_le as the node realization; it is related substrate but not the same theorem shape or conclusion, and its chi-square-to-TV constant only gives the estimator-risk form. Define a local testing lemma from total variation and chi-square/product chi-square, or reuse a genuine testing-error lemma if one exists. The existing Causalean decl may still be used as inspiration or for a different real-estimation reduction, but it does not soundly realize this core node as stated.

perItemFindings.label: S2
perItemFindings.one_line: `Causalean.Stat.iid_two_point_lower_bound` is an n-sample real-estimator miss-probability theorem over `Measure.pi`, not a world/sampling-model object hosting the S2 symbols `M_n`, `U_n`, `h_n`, `q_n`, `B_n` or the bespoke minimax/feasible regret functionals.

review.verbatim_critique: Re-plan S2: keep the i.i.d. experiment encoded directly as `Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin n => P)` and cite `iid_two_point_lower_bound` only as analogous/minimax substrate if a real-estimator miss-probability reduction is actually used. The plan already correctly drops direct reuse of estimator-risk two-point lemmas for the per-test error floor, so S2 should not declare `Causalean.Stat.iid_two_point_lower_bound` as the reused world for this policy-regret sampling block.

reason: Case 7 (producer drift at 1.5) + regression pattern (b) different flags each round, none persisting — all three rejects are plan-level mechanical/reuse-fit issues (P4 member-closure, then two reuse-fit mismatches), with the .tex/.md math coherent throughout; no upstream derivation defect. Latest flag is S2 declaring iid_two_point_lower_bound as the reused 'world' object when it is an n-sample estimator-risk theorem, not a sampling-model host for M_n/U_n/q_n. This is NL/plan artifact drift fixable in plan.json. Matches per-boundary heuristic (plan_gate + reuse-fit → stage_1).
proposed_action: Re-run nl-formalizer in local-fix mode on plan.json: (1) for S2, encode the i.i.d. experiment directly as `Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin n => P)` and cite `Causalean.Stat.iid_two_point_lower_bound` ONLY as analogous/minimax substrate (not as the reused world hosting M_n, U_n, h_n, q_n, B_n or the bespoke minimax/feasible regret functionals); (2) preserve the prior round's fixes — lem:witness-membership listing bundled class def:law-class for the six member-assumption deps, and lem:le-cam-two-point-chisq realized as the local two-hypothesis testing-error floor rather than the estimator-risk decl. perItemFindings.label: lem:le-cam-two-point-chisq
perItemFindings.one_line: The cited decl Causalean.Stat.two_point_lower_bound_of_chiSqDiv_le is a real-valued estimator miss-probability bound with parameters est, θ₀, θ₁, s and conclusion on max miss probability; the core node is a binary testing lemma giving a summed test-error lower bound from chi-square/product control.

review.verbatim_critique: Re-plan lem:le-cam-two-point-chisq as a local thin testing lemma, not direct reuse of Causalean.Stat.two_point_lower_bound_of_chiSqDiv_le. Build it from the closer substrate facts Causalean.Stat.one_sub_tvDist_le_test plus Causalean.Stat.tvDist_le_half_sqrt_chiSqDiv and Causalean.Stat.chiSqDiv_prod / product chi-square control, or explicitly record an adapter from binary tests to real estimators if using the existing estimator lemma. The other inspected bypasses/reuse targets are at the intended abstraction: observed-law PO/AIPW bypass is deliberate, and localized-VC items are explicit Prop assumptions with Causalean envelope lemmas only as discharge substrate.

report_summary: Verified no direct reuse dispositions; define-local/bypass choices are sound, cited Causalean TV/chi-square/VC substrates fit the local-wrapper roles and are not sorry-backed, and the sole Defer node is genuinely open tightness rather than mis-tagged substrate.

review.notes: Verified no direct reuse dispositions; define-local/bypass choices are sound, cited Causalean TV/chi-square/VC substrates fit the local-wrapper roles and are not sorry-backed, and the sole Defer node is genuinely open tightness rather than mis-tagged substrate.

perItemFindings.label: ass:iid
perItemFindings.one_line: The plan marks `ass:iid` as define-local `IsIIDSample`, but the library has exact cluster primitive `Causalean.Stat.IIDSample Ω X μ P` with fields `Z`, measurability, mutual independence, identical distribution, and law match; the current local plan only says it follows the pattern, and the scaffolded `IsIIDSample P := IsProbabilityMeasure P.dataMeasure` does not state i.i.d. sampling at all.

review.verbatim_critique: Re-plan `ass:iid`/the S1 sampling binding to reuse `Causalean.Stat.IIDSample` directly for sample-level arguments, or explicitly remove the sample assumption and formulate only product-law statements with `Measure.pi`. Do not define a local `IsIIDSample` that merely says the one-observation law is a probability measure; that misses the core's i.i.d. sampling content and rebuilds an exact existing substrate primitive.

report_summary: Verified reuse soundness for S4/ass:iid via Causalean.Stat.IIDSample, the Le Cam substrate via one_sub_tvDist_le_test/tvDist_le_half_sqrt_chiSqDiv/chiSqDiv_prod, and localized VC substrate references; no reuse type-fit, abstraction, world-soundness, define-local, defer-tag, or unwitting sorry-backed dependency issue found.

review.notes: Verified reuse soundness for S4/ass:iid via Causalean.Stat.IIDSample, the Le Cam substrate via one_sub_tvDist_le_test/tvDist_le_half_sqrt_chiSqDiv/chiSqDiv_prod, and localized VC substrate references; no reuse type-fit, abstraction, world-soundness, define-local, defer-tag, or unwitting sorry-backed dependency issue found.

perItemFindings.label: ass:vc-localized-envelope
perItemFindings.one_line: This is a localized finite-VC/Rademacher empirical-process theorem, not a modeling assumption; the plan even names Causalean.Stat.Concentration.vcLocalizedEnvelope / vcLocalizedRademacherUpperBound / localized_uniform_deviation_sharp as reuse targets but keeps the node as lean_kind:"assumption", disposition:"define-local", defer_tier:false, so the substrate fact is silently assumed instead of discharged or escalated.

perItemFindings.label: ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope
perItemFindings.one_line: This offset/Rademacher bound is substrate empirical-process mathematics; the plan cites Causalean.Stat.Concentration.localized_uniform_deviation_sharp / vcLocalizedEnvelope as the analytic source but leaves it as a plain Prop hypothesis used by oeq:feasible-upper and helper lemmas, with no reuse discharge and no defer_tier substrate gate.

review.verbatim_critique: Re-plan the two empirical-process assumption nodes. If the cited Causalean localized-VC machinery actually proves the exact fixed-radius and offset-envelope statements needed here, reclassify them as derived lemmas with disposition:"reuse" (or thin local wrappers over those Causalean theorems) and remove them from theorem hyps. If the cited decls are only nearby and do not cover the exact offset/fixed-radius claims, mark the missing empirical-process content as defer_tier:true substrate-gate rather than carrying it as ordinary assumptions. The IIDSample reuse for S4/ass:iid is sound: Causalean.Stat.IIDSample has the advertised Z/meas/indep/identDist/law fields and is at the right sampling-model abstraction.

report_summary: Verified reuse soundness for S4/ass:iid and the Le Cam chi-square/TV substrate, confirmed no sorry-backed reused decls in the cited substrate, and checked define-local/defer choices including PO/AIPW bypasses and localized-VC substrate gates against the nearby Causalean signatures.

perItemFindings.label: ass:vc-localized-envelope
perItemFindings.one_line: This is a localized finite-VC empirical-process theorem, not a modeling assumption; the plan keeps it as define-local Prop hypothesis even though Causalean.Stat.Concentration.localized_uniform_deviation_expectation plus vcLocalizedEnvelope are present, axiom-clean, and match the fixed-radius in-expectation envelope substrate the node needs.

perItemFindings.label: ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope
perItemFindings.one_line: This is an offset/Rademacher empirical-process theorem, not a modeling assumption; the plan keeps it as define-local Prop hypothesis even though Causalean.Stat.Concentration.localized_offset_expectation is present, axiom-clean, and states the self-localizing offset positive-part envelope the node needs after the margin-coupling bridge.

review.verbatim_critique: Re-plan ass:vc-localized-envelope and ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope. They cannot remain plain assumptions merely because the deterministic kind gate wanted assumption-kind coverage: under the substrate-fact policy they must either be discharged as derived reuse lemmas from the Causalean localized-envelope substrate (localized_uniform_deviation_expectation/vcLocalizedEnvelope and localized_offset_expectation) and then dropped from consumers' hyps, or explicitly marked defer_tier substrate-gates if the bridge lemmas are not yet built. The ass:iid/S4 reuse of Causalean.Stat.IIDSample is type-fit and sound.

reason: Case 4b/reuse-fit + regression pattern (b/oscillation): plan-layer deadlock — deterministic P3 gate demands lean_kind=assumption on the ass: core nodes while the substrate-fact reviewer demands they be reuse-discharged; producer ping-ponged between the two states, satisfying one gate per round. Per-boundary heuristic (plan_gate + reuse-fit flags → stage_1) applies. Substrate confirmed present/axiom-clean (LocalizedEnvelopeExpectation.lean, VCLocalizedRegime.lean), so not Case 1/user; .tex is internally consistent (assumptions are user-approved with a documented discharge target), so not stage_0.
proposed_action: Re-plan only the two nodes ass:vc-localized-envelope and ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope in plan.json: encode each as a DERIVED REUSE node over the axiom-clean Causalean substrate (ass:vc-localized-envelope ← Causalean.Stat.Concentration.localized_uniform_deviation_expectation / vcLocalizedEnvelope; ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope ← Causalean.Stat.Concentration.localized_offset_expectation) using the reuse-discharge kind the deterministic gate accepts (NOT lean_kind:lemma on a core-assumption node, which trips P3, and NOT plain lean_kind:assumption, which trips FLAG-substrate-assumed), and DROP both from the hyps of every consumer (oeq:feasible-upper, lem:crude-localized-master-bound, lem:localized-vc-process-bound, lem:crossfit-localized-process-reduction, lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control) so P4 hyp-coverage is satisfied by the discharge rather than by re-listing. If the margin-coupling bridge lemmas required to invoke localized_offset_expectation are not yet built, instead mark that node defer_tier substrate-gate (per the round-2 reviewer's second option) rather than re-adding it as a plain assumption. Keep every other core node mapped exactly once.
cite: FLAG-substrate-assumed @ ass:vc-localized-envelope, ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope; P3 lean_kind invalid for core assumption
